name: Video Watch Assistant
description: Watches a mapped local video stream, detects configured visual targets, and reports count, label, category, color, position, activity, and alert status for review.

alert_policy.description: Cooldown, confidence, and notification policy for reviewer notices.

attention_instruction.description: Operator instruction for a detail to watch in the current stream.
attention_instruction.example: Pay attention to the red backpack near the left doorway.

slack_destination.description: Optional approved Slack destination for alert delivery.

video_source.description: Approved RTSP stream or bundled demo feed.

visual_targets.description: Configured subjects, objects, or activities to detect.

contract.summary: User RTSP sources can be unstable or unavailable inside OpenShell, so this blueprint maps any validated upstream video or demo file into one stable local stream endpoint.
users: Video operators, security reviewers, safety operations teams, and site-access coordinators.

steps.goal: Validate the mapped stream source and start the monitoring session.
steps.label: Start Video Monitor

steps.goal: Emit frame ticks according to the configured sample interval.
steps.label: Sample Video Frames

steps.goal: Analyze sampled frames for configured targets and alert status.
steps.label: Detect Visual Targets

steps.goal: Write the current visual detection review artifact and notice events.
steps.label: Write Watch Summary

primary.role: AI assistant for reviewing video stream activity and preparing visual detection alerts.

rules.message: video_source.uri must be an rtsp:// or rtsps:// URL.
rules.name: video_source_uri_rtsp

metadata.agent_role: AI assistant for reviewing video stream activity and preparing visual detection alerts.
metadata.customizable_for: Video monitoring, configurable visual detection, facility operations, and site safety workflows.
metadata.description: Watches a mapped local video stream, detects configured visual targets, and reports count, label, category, color, position, activity, and alert status for review.

human_control.reason: The workflow surfaces notable visual changes to reviewers but does not execute physical security actions by itself.

fields.description: Leave the default mapped endpoint unless using an approved RTSP stream.
fields.label: Video input

fields.description: Base URL for the vision-language model server.
fields.label: VL model URL

fields.description: Vision-language model used to understand sampled frames.
fields.label: VL model name

init_config_review.instruction: When running in OtterDesk, show these overwriteable fields during init config and ask the user to change them or keep the defaults before launch.

input_contract.description: User RTSP sources can be unstable or unavailable inside OpenShell, so this blueprint maps any validated upstream video or demo file into one stable local stream endpoint.

optional_inputs.description: Operator instruction for a detail to watch in the current stream.
optional_inputs.example: Pay attention to the red backpack near the left doorway.
optional_inputs.name: attention_instruction

optional_inputs.description: Optional approved Slack destination for alert delivery.
optional_inputs.name: slack_destination

connector.description: Approved camera stream metadata supplied by a site video connector.

file.description: JSON object loaded from a local file path.

json.description: Inline JSON object supplied through config or runtime overrides.

live_stream.description: Runtime stream configuration for the mapped local RTSP endpoint.

mock.description: Bundled demo video loop and mock VLM detection path.

required_inputs.description: Approved RTSP stream or bundled demo feed.
required_inputs.name: video_source

required_inputs.description: Configured subjects, objects, or activities to detect.
required_inputs.name: visual_targets

required_inputs.description: Cooldown, confidence, and notification policy for reviewer notices.
required_inputs.name: alert_policy

validation_rules.message: video_source.uri must be an rtsp:// or rtsps:// URL.
validation_rules.name: video_source_uri_rtsp

artifacts.description: Stable run-store files and domain-specific outputs.

human.description: Human notices, approval requests, responses, timeouts, and applied decisions.

input_skills.description: Connector-backed or reusable skill-backed input sources.

inputs.description: Resolved runtime inputs and adapter provenance.

logs.description: Runtime stdout/stderr and explicit log artifacts.

output_skills.description: Optional fan-out destinations after local run-store writes.

outputs.description: Primary result and final user-facing artifact.

resources.description: Resource samples such as token, runtime, connector, and disk usage.

streams.description: Named data or event streams exposed by the blueprint.

llm.agent_role: AI assistant for reviewing video stream activity and preparing visual detection alerts.

metadata.llm_agent_role: AI assistant for reviewing video stream activity and preparing visual detection alerts.
metadata.name: Video Watch Assistant
metadata.output: Visual detection observations with count, label, category, color, position, activity, confidence, and alert status.

final_artifact.description: The durable user-facing artifact should summarize the result, recommendation, confidence, evidence, next steps, and source references.

output_contract.stdout: single JSON object, event envelope, or bundle path

metadata.problem_solved: User RTSP sources can be unstable or unavailable inside OpenShell, so this blueprint maps any validated upstream video or demo file into one stable local stream endpoint.

quick_test.description: Quick test mode shortens the sample interval, disables Slack, and uses mock visual detection unless OLLAMA_LIVE_IN_QUICK_TEST=1.

metadata.simulation_type: Video stream sampling with visual detection review state.

standard.execution_model: load_metadata -> resolve_config -> load_inputs -> start_run_store -> observe_simulation_state -> call_llm_agent -> evaluate_workflow_state -> route_by_context -> request_human_input -> apply_decision_to_simulation -> emit_events -> write_final_artifact -> fan_out_output_skills

metadata.target_user: Video operators, security reviewers, safety operations teams, and site-access coordinators.
metadata.target_users: Video operators, security reviewers, safety operations teams, and site-access coordinators.

video_source.description: Default mapped RTSP endpoint consumed by the OpenShell worker. The host-side pre-launch hook loops the bundled demo video into the selected local RTSP port before launch.

arguments.browser_publish_source: Set to disabled because this blueprint's host-side mapper owns publishing into the local RTSP endpoint.
arguments.browser_video_source: Resolved by scripts/pre-launch.sh. The hook starts MediaMTX, selects an available browser preview port, and writes the preview URL into launch config.
arguments.run_id: MirrorNeuron run id used to resolve ~/.mn/runs/<run_id>.
arguments.url: Shared support Gradio app URL written to web_ui.json.

web_ui.description: Shared blueprint support Gradio dashboard rendered from the blueprint's Grafana JSON model and live run-store events.
